worth_it_explanation: Yahoo DSP applies baseline bot avoidance through its Inventory Quality Targeting controls, but that baseline does not remove the need to confirm those controls are switched on, to reconcile measured invalid traffic rates against billed impressions, or to identify and exclude supply sources with persistently elevated fraud exposure. These checks are conducted inside the Yahoo DSP line interface and, where a third-party verification vendor is connected, inside the vendor's dashboard. The work is inspection and list management, not a rebuild, and the fixes take effect from the next impression served.
intro: Yahoo DSP provides Inventory Quality Targeting controls at the line level, including a bot avoidance setting that blocks known fraud and malware sources before a bid is placed. That pre-bid layer addresses general invalid traffic (GIVT), meaning traffic identified through routine means such as known bot lists, data center signatures, and crawler patterns. Sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT), which mimics real user behavior and requires advanced behavioral analysis to detect, is harder to filter at the platform level and may require a third-party verification vendor's pre-bid segment for additional coverage. These checks confirm that Yahoo DSP's native bot avoidance controls are active on each line, that measured invalid traffic rates from a verification vendor are reconciled against delivery data, and that supply sources showing persistently elevated fraud exposure are identified and blocked.

audit_checks.title: Bot avoidance in Inventory Quality Targeting is not confirmed active on each line
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Yahoo DSP exposes a bot avoidance control within Inventory Quality Targeting at the line level. When that control is left off, the line may serve impressions on known fraud and malware sources that the platform would otherwise block before bidding. Confirming the setting is on for each active line is a short check with a direct effect on whether the buyer pays for known non-human traffic.

educate.base:
Yahoo DSP includes an Inventory Quality Targeting section at the line level. Within that section, a bot avoidance control blocks bid requests from known fraud and malware sources before the impression is served. Yahoo evaluates inbound requests and removes those that match known non-human signatures before the line places a bid. The buyer does not pay for impressions on sources that trigger the filter.

General invalid traffic (GIVT), as defined by the Media Rating Council (MRC), covers traffic identified through routine means: known bot and spider lists, data-center IP ranges, and standardized parameter checks. The bot avoidance control in Yahoo DSP's Inventory Quality Targeting addresses this GIVT layer. Sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT), which the MRC defines as non-human activity requiring advanced analytics and behavioral corroboration to detect, is harder to eliminate at the platform level alone.

The bot avoidance control is set at the line level, not inherited from the campaign. A line created without reviewing the Inventory Quality Targeting section may have the control off. Because the hierarchy in Yahoo DSP runs Advertiser, then Campaign, then Line, then Ad, each line needs to be checked individually.

A few signs point to the bot avoidance control being inactive:
  • Lines were created from a template or duplicated from an older line, and the Inventory Quality Targeting section was not reviewed during setup
  • The account has no record of a quality-control step covering Inventory Quality Targeting settings for active lines
  • A verification vendor's fraud exposure report shows elevated invalid traffic on lines that were expected to have pre-bid protection
You may want to open each active line's targeting settings and confirm the bot avoidance control in the Inventory Quality Targeting section is switched on before drawing conclusions from delivery data.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser, impressions served to known bots inflate delivery counts without contributing to product consideration or conversion signals. You may want to prioritize confirming the bot avoidance setting on lines driving traffic to product or category pages, where inflated impression counts can distort cost-per-click and cost-per-acquisition calculations.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, bot traffic on display or video lines inflates impression and potentially click metrics without producing form submissions. You may want to confirm the bot avoidance control is active on lines with low conversion rates before attributing the underperformance to targeting or creative.

learn_more.source_title: Yahoo Buying Guide: Inventory Quality Targeting and Bot Avoidance
MRC Invalid Traffic Detection and Filtration Standards and Guidelines

investigate.intro: Open each active line in Yahoo DSP and confirm the bot avoidance control within Inventory Quality Targeting is switched on.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: In Yahoo DSP, select the Advertisers icon on the left pane and navigate to the advertiser you are auditing.
On the Campaigns page, open each active campaign and navigate to the Lines list view.
For each active line, open the line and navigate to the Targeting page.

In the Targeting page, locate the Inventory Quality Targeting section. Confirm that the Bot avoidance control is switched on. Bot avoidance blocks bid requests from known fraud and malware sources before the impression is served.
Record the line name and whether the Bot avoidance control is on or off. Flag any line where it is off, particularly lines buying from open auction inventory.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, for each active line, whether the bot avoidance control in the Inventory Quality Targeting section is switched on. A line with the control on passes. A line with the control off, particularly one buying from open auction inventory, is a fail recorded with the line name and the campaign it belongs to. Lines that buy exclusively from registered private marketplace deals with known publishers where inventory quality has been separately verified may be noted as lower priority, but the finding should still be recorded.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the bot avoidance status for each active line above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more of {{site_name}}'s active Yahoo DSP lines have the bot avoidance control in Inventory Quality Targeting switched off. Yahoo DSP's bot avoidance filters bid requests from known fraud and malware sources before the impression is served, which means lines running without it may pay for general invalid traffic (GIVT) that the platform would otherwise block. {{voice_recommend}} opening each active line's Targeting page, confirming the bot avoidance control is on in the Inventory Quality Targeting section, and establishing a line-setup checklist that includes this step so new lines are not created without it.

audit_checks.title: Measured invalid traffic rates from a verification vendor are not reconciled against billed impressions
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A verification vendor's invalid traffic measurement report tells the buyer what share of delivered impressions were flagged as invalid after serving. Without reconciling that rate against the impression counts Yahoo DSP billed, the buyer has no way to determine whether the measured invalid traffic represents a significant portion of spend or a negligible one, and no basis for requesting credit or adjusting controls.

educate.base:
Pre-bid bot avoidance reduces the volume of known invalid traffic before bidding, but it does not eliminate all invalid traffic from delivered impressions. Post-bid measurement by a third-party verification vendor captures what reaches delivery: the vendor tags or measures the impression after it is served and scores it against its invalid traffic detection methodology.

The Media Rating Council (MRC) distinguishes between GIVT, which platforms filter using routine list-based methods, and sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT), which requires advanced analytics and behavioral corroboration to detect. A verification vendor's post-bid measurement report reflects the vendor's view of both GIVT and SIVT that reached delivery despite pre-bid filtering. Because the vendor's detection methodology goes beyond what platform-level pre-bid filtering addresses, the post-bid report often surfaces invalid traffic that the platform's own controls did not catch.

Reconciling the vendor's measured invalid traffic rate against Yahoo DSP's billed impression count converts a percentage into an absolute number of impressions, which in turn converts to a dollar figure. That calculation tells the buyer whether the measured invalid traffic is material. A 3% invalid traffic rate on a 10,000-impression line item is a different situation from a 3% rate on a 2,000,000-impression line item. Some verification vendors and DSPs offer contractual arrangements for invalid traffic credits when measured rates exceed a defined threshold.

A few signs point to invalid traffic rates not being reconciled:
  • A verification vendor is connected to the account, but no one on the team reviews the vendor's fraud exposure report alongside Yahoo DSP's billing data on a regular cadence
  • The account has no documented threshold for what constitutes an actionable invalid traffic rate
  • Credits or adjustments for measured invalid traffic have never been requested or discussed with the Yahoo DSP account team
You may want to pull the verification vendor's invalid traffic report for the last 30 days and compare the flagged impression count against Yahoo DSP's billed total for the same period before deciding whether the rate warrants further action.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser, reconciling invalid traffic rates at the line level lets you identify whether specific product or category campaign lines are delivering a disproportionate share of invalid impressions. You may want to cross-reference the vendor's fraud report with the lines driving the highest spend to determine whether the invalid traffic exposure is concentrated in high-budget placements.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, invalid traffic reconciliation is relevant to any line where low conversion rates have been attributed to creative or targeting. You may want to check whether a verification vendor's report shows elevated invalid traffic on underperforming lines before concluding that the audience targeting is the problem.

learn_more.source_title: MRC Invalid Traffic Detection and Filtration Standards and Guidelines
Yahoo Buying Guide: Inventory Quality Targeting and Bot Avoidance

educate.learn_more_note: Yahoo DSP's own advertiser-facing help documentation on invalid traffic reporting and credit processes is accessible through the Yahoo DSP platform login. Ask your Yahoo DSP account team for documentation on the platform's invalid traffic credit policy and vendor reconciliation process.

investigate.intro: Pull the verification vendor's invalid traffic report for the last 30 days and compare the flagged impression count against Yahoo DSP's billed total for the same period.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: In Yahoo DSP, navigate to the reporting section for the advertiser you are auditing. Pull a delivery report for the last 30 days filtered to the lines you are reviewing. Note the total billed impressions per line.

Log in to the connected verification vendor's platform (e.g., DoubleVerify, Integral Ad Science, or Oracle Moat). Pull the fraud or invalid traffic exposure report for the same 30-day period and the same lines.
For each line, record the vendor's measured invalid traffic rate and calculate the absolute number of invalid impressions by applying the rate to the Yahoo DSP billed impression count.
Note any line where the measured invalid traffic rate exceeds 3% of billed impressions, or where the absolute count of invalid impressions represents a material portion of the line's budget.
Check whether your contract with Yahoo DSP or the verification vendor includes a threshold for invalid traffic credits. If it does, compare the measured rates against that threshold.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, for each reviewed line, the Yahoo DSP billed impression count, the verification vendor's measured invalid traffic rate, and the calculated absolute count of invalid impressions. A line where the invalid traffic rate is below the account's defined threshold, or where the rate has been reviewed and accepted, passes. A line where the measured invalid traffic rate exceeds the threshold and no credit has been requested or action taken is a fail recorded with the line name, the measured rate, and the absolute impression count.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the billed impression count and the verification vendor's measured invalid traffic rate for each line above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP account does not have a documented process for reconciling the verification vendor's measured invalid traffic rates against billed impressions. Without this reconciliation, the account cannot determine whether measured invalid traffic represents a material share of spend, and has no basis for requesting credit or adjusting controls. {{voice_recommend}} establishing a monthly reconciliation cadence in which the verification vendor's fraud exposure report is compared against Yahoo DSP's billed totals at the line level, defining a threshold rate that triggers a credit request or a targeting adjustment, and documenting the outcome of each review.

audit_checks.title: Supply sources with persistently elevated invalid traffic rates are not identified and blocked
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A supply source that delivers a high share of invalid traffic on one flight will typically produce the same pattern on the next. The underlying traffic quality of that source has not changed. Without identifying and blocking those sources, the account continues allocating budget to inventory that has already demonstrated it does not deliver real audiences.

educate.base:
Pre-bid bot avoidance and post-bid reconciliation address the volume of invalid traffic reaching delivery, but they do not prevent the same problematic supply sources from appearing in future bid auctions. A supply source that repeatedly delivers high invalid traffic rates warrants a direct targeting exclusion so the account's lines do not bid on its inventory going forward.

In Yahoo DSP, supply source exclusions are applied at the line level through the Targeting page, where specific app or site targets can be blocked. A line can block individual app or site identifiers. Exclusions added at the line level apply to that line on all future flights without needing to be reapplied each time the campaign refreshes.

Identifying which supply sources to exclude requires placement-level or app-level invalid traffic data from a verification vendor. The vendor's report breaks down invalid traffic rates by domain or app, which surfaces the sources contributing the most invalid impressions. A source consistently showing an invalid traffic rate above the account's defined threshold across multiple reporting periods is a reasonable candidate for a permanent block.

The MRC's invalid traffic detection and filtration standards distinguish between GIVT, filtered through routine means, and SIVT, which requires advanced analytics. A source generating primarily GIVT at a high rate may indicate a low-quality or fraudulent inventory environment that pre-bid filtering was not catching for that specific source. A source generating SIVT may indicate a more deliberate fraud operation targeting the account's campaign types.

A few signs point to high-invalid-traffic supply sources remaining in the active bid pool:
  • The verification vendor's fraud report shows one or more app or site identifiers with invalid traffic rates above 5% across two or more consecutive reporting periods
  • The account's line exclusion lists have not been updated since the campaign launched
  • The same app or site identifiers appear in the invalid traffic report repeatedly without any corresponding block applied to the lines buying from them
You may want to review the verification vendor's placement-level invalid traffic report for the last 60 days and compare the highest-rate sources against the current exclusion settings on each active line before building the block list.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser, in-app and open auction supply sources with high invalid traffic rates are worth reviewing alongside the click-to-session match rate in the site analytics tool. A source generating high impressions and clicks in Yahoo DSP that does not produce corresponding sessions in the analytics tool is a strong signal that the traffic is not reaching real users.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, persistently high invalid traffic on specific supply sources is relevant to any line where form submission rates are low. You may want to compare the sources flagged in the verification vendor's invalid traffic report against the lines with the lowest conversion rates to determine whether the two overlap.

learn_more.source_title: Yahoo Buying Guide: Inventory Quality Targeting and Bot Avoidance
MRC Invalid Traffic Detection and Filtration Standards and Guidelines

educate.learn_more_note: Yahoo DSP's advertiser-facing documentation on site and app blocking within the Inventory Quality Targeting controls is accessible through the Yahoo DSP platform login. Ask your Yahoo DSP account team for guidance on applying line-level exclusions for specific supply sources.

investigate.intro: Pull the verification vendor's placement-level invalid traffic report for the last 60 days and identify supply sources with persistently elevated rates, then check whether those sources are excluded on the relevant lines.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Log in to the connected verification vendor's platform and pull the fraud or invalid traffic report broken down by app or site for the last 60 days. Filter to the Yahoo DSP lines you are auditing.
Identify any app or site identifiers where the invalid traffic rate exceeds 5% across the reporting period, or that appear in the high-invalid-traffic segment for two or more consecutive months.
In Yahoo DSP, navigate to each active line that showed elevated invalid traffic. Open the line's Targeting page and review the current exclusion settings for apps and sites.
For each flagged app or site identifier, confirm whether it is already blocked on the relevant lines. Record any flagged source that is not currently excluded.

If no verification vendor is connected to the account, note this as a separate gap. Without placement-level invalid traffic data, the account has no mechanism for identifying which supply sources are generating invalid traffic beyond Yahoo DSP's aggregate delivery report.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, for each flagged supply source, the measured invalid traffic rate, the number of reporting periods in which it appeared above the threshold, and whether it is currently blocked on the lines buying from it. A flagged source that is excluded on all relevant lines, or where the rate has been reviewed and the source retained with documented rationale, passes. A flagged source with a persistently elevated invalid traffic rate that is not excluded and has no documented review is a fail recorded with the app or site identifier, the measured rate, and the lines where it remains in the active bid pool.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the flagged supply sources and their invalid traffic rates alongside the current exclusion status on each relevant line above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP lines include supply sources with persistently elevated invalid traffic rates that have not been excluded from future bidding. These sources have appeared in the verification vendor's fraud exposure report across multiple reporting periods without a corresponding block being applied to the lines buying from them, meaning the same low-quality inventory continues consuming budget on each new flight. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the verification vendor's placement-level invalid traffic report for the last 60 days, applying line-level blocks in Yahoo DSP's Targeting page for each source with a rate above the account's defined threshold, and establishing a regular review cadence so new high-invalid-traffic sources are caught and excluded before they accumulate significant spend.
